name: Castro Candaz Route
name_original: Embalse de Belesar – circular por la Ribeira Sacra
description: Hike the moderate Castro Candaz Route in Ribeira Sacra, a 6.4-mile trail with reservoir views and a submerged historical site.
region_intro: Hiking around Taboada, located within Spain's Ribeira Sacra region, offers diverse landscapes for outdoor enthusiasts. The area features paths winding through ancient forests and picturesque vineyards, characteristic of the region. Trails often provide scenic views of rivers, such as the Loio River, and reservoirs. Historical elements, including submerged sites and the historic Taboada Bridge, are integrated into the natural setting, adding cultural interest to the exploration. Best hiking trails in Taboada The most popular hiking route is Castro Candaz Route, a 6.4 miles (10.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 57 minutes to complete, offering views of a reservoir and a historical site. Another top favourite among local hikers is Loio River and Belesar Loop, a moderate 9.3 miles (14.9 km) path. This route provides expansive views of the Loio River and the Belesar area. Local hikers also love the Chapel of Our Lady of Faro – Camino de Santiago Waymarker loop from Galicia, a 17.2 miles (27.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 8 hours 10 minutes. Hiking around Taboada is defined by ancient forests, vineyards, and river views, often incorporating historical landmarks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging treks. The routes in Taboada are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 600 hikers have used komoot to explore Taboada's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Taboada?
A: Taboada offers a diverse selection of over 20 hiking trails. These routes cater to various fitness levels, ranging from easy walks to more challenging treks, ensuring there's something for every outdoor enthusiast.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Taboada?
A: Yes, Taboada features 6 easy hiking routes. These trails typically take up to two hours to complete with minimal elevation gain, making them ideal for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. They are suitable for all skill levels.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Taboada?
A: Hiking in Taboada offers a rich variety of landscapes. You can expect to traverse paths winding through ancient forests and picturesque vineyards, which are characteristic of the Ribeira Sacra region. Many trails also provide scenic views of rivers, such as the Loio River, and expansive reservoirs.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Taboada?
A: Absolutely. The 6 easy routes in Taboada are generally suitable for families. These trails have minimal elevation gain and shorter durations, making them a great way to explore the natural beauty of the region with children.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Taboada?
A: While specific rules for each trail may vary, generally, dogs are welcome on many hiking trails in Taboada. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near agricultural land, and to carry waste bags to clean up after them.

Q: What interesting landmarks or historical sites can I see along the trails?
A: Taboada's trails often integrate historical elements into the natural setting. You might encounter the historic 10th-century Taboada Bridge, which is still used by pilgrims on the Silver Way of the Camino de Santiago. Other notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/751631" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portomarín Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/770706" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castromaior Archaeological Site&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/137061" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of St John of Portomarín&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979352603/castro-candaz-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro Candaz Route&lt;/a&gt;, for example, offers views of a reservoir and a submerged historical site.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Taboada?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Taboada are designed as loops, offering circular routes that bring you back to your starting point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980846554/loio-river-and-belesar-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loio River and Belesar Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which provides expansive views of the river and surrounding area.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Taboada?
A: The Ribeira Sacra region, including Taboada, is generally pleasant for hiking from spring through autumn. Spring brings lush greenery and blooming flora, while autumn offers vibrant fall colors in the vineyards and forests. Summers are typically warm, and winters can be cooler and wetter, though hiking is possible year-round depending on weather conditions.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads in Taboada?
A: Parking availability can vary by trailhead. In many rural areas of Galicia, including Taboada, you can often find informal parking spots near the start of popular routes or in nearby villages. For specific routes, it's advisable to check the route details on komoot for information on designated parking areas.

Q: Do I need any permits to hike in Taboada or the Ribeira Sacra region?
A: Generally, you do not need specific permits for day hiking on established trails in Taboada or the broader Ribeira Sacra region. Most trails are freely accessible. However, always respect private property and any local signage you may encounter.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Taboada?
A: The hiking experiences in Taboada are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, including ancient forests and vineyards, the scenic river and reservoir views, and the integration of historical landmarks into the natural setting. The variety of trails catering to different ability levels is also frequently appreciated.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the hiking trails in Taboada?
A: Yes, many trails in Taboada offer stunning viewpoints, particularly those overlooking the rivers and reservoirs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5613861" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Ribeira Sacra and Serras do Oribio and Courel Biosphere Reserve&lt;/a&gt; is a notable highlight, providing expansive vistas of the region's natural beauty.
tips: El Castro Candaz are the remains of a pre-Roman fortress and later medieval fortress, located between the mouth of the Lama River and the River Envionde on the Miño River, in the parish of Pedrafita (Chantada, Lugo). It is currently flooded by the waters of the embalse de Belesar.

faqs.question: Where does the Castro Candaz Route start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The Castro Candaz Route is a circular trail, and while specific trailhead information isn't provided, it's located in the Ribeira Sacra region. Hikers typically find parking near the starting points in the local villages that access the route. Given its popularity, especially when the Castro Candaz emerges, arriving earlier in the day might help secure a spot.

faqs.question: What is the typical duration to complete the Castro Candaz Route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 10.3 kilometers (6.4 miles) long. Most hikers can expect to complete it in about 2 hours and 57 minutes, depending on their pace, stops for photos, and exploration of the historical sites.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Castro Candaz Route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied, offering a dynamic hiking experience. You'll encounter comfortable paths, dirt tracks, and potentially muddy sections, especially after rain. There are both descents and ascents, with some parts featuring steep slopes and uneven volcanic rock. Good gripping trail shoes are essential for navigating these diverse surfaces.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers stunning panoramic views of the Miño River and the expansive Belesar reservoir. You'll walk through picturesque vineyards, many terraced into the hillsides (known as 'socalcos'), and past traditional bodegas. Dense forests of oaks and chestnuts also line parts of the route, and you'll spot architectural heritage like stone houses, 'pazos', and a carved stone 'cruceiro' in Mosteiro. The ultimate highlight is the historical site of Castro Candaz itself.

faqs.question: What is the significance of Castro Candaz and when can it be seen?
faqs.answer: The Castro Candaz consists of the remains of a Celtic settlement and a medieval fortress. It's uniquely located at the confluence of the Lama and Enviande rivers with the Miño. Due to the construction of the Belesar dam, it is usually submerged beneath the reservoir's waters. However, during periods of low water levels, often caused by drought, the remains emerge, offering a rare and captivating glimpse into history.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike the Castro Candaz Route largely depends on what you want to see. To witness the Castro Candaz emerging from the reservoir, you'll need to visit during periods of low water levels, which often occur during droughts. Otherwise, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, while summer can be warm but provides longer daylight hours.

faqs.question: Is the Castro Candaz Route suitable for families or children?
faqs.answer: The route is generally considered to have a moderate difficulty level. While it offers diverse scenery, some sections include steep slopes and uneven, potentially slippery volcanic rock. There are options for different paths, including a shorter, steeper route with stone stairs. Families with younger children or those less accustomed to hiking might find certain parts challenging, so assess your group's fitness level carefully.

faqs.question: Is the Castro Candaz Route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Castro Candaz Route is generally dog-friendly. However, given the varied terrain, including potentially muddy sections, steep slopes, and uneven surfaces, ensure your dog is accustomed to such conditions. Always keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and respect other hikers, especially when passing through vineyards or near livestock.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for hiking in Ribeira Sacra?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits or entrance fees required to hike the Castro Candaz Route or other trails within the Ribeira Sacra region. It's a natural area open for public enjoyment. However, always respect private property, especially when passing through vineyards or near traditional bodegas.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a hike on the Castro Candaz Route?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and potential for slippery surfaces, good gripping trail shoes are essential. We also recommend bringing sufficient water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and layers of clothing as weather conditions can change. A camera is a must for the stunning views, and trekking poles can be helpful for stability on steeper sections.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The Castro Candaz Route is located in the Ribeira Sacra region, which has several charming villages. You can find local cafes, pubs, and various accommodation options (including rural guesthouses and hotels) in nearby towns like Chantada or other communities within the region. It's a good idea to check in advance for specific opening hours, especially outside of peak season.
